scope.summary: Implement a post-simulation garment sculpt tool that applies smooth brush-based displacement to the simulated mesh vertices (push/pull/smooth/relax), re-runs UV unfurl for affected panels, and stores the sculpt delta as an additive override layer on the garment authority row
acceptance_criteria: SculptBrush type (mode: Push | Pull | Smooth | Relax, radius_mm: f32, strength: f32) is defined in tailor-solver/src/sculpt/brush.rs
apply_brush(brush, hit_point_mm, mesh) produces a per-vertex displacement delta and blends it with existing sculpt delta on the garment row
After sculpt, ARAP unfurl is re-run for panels whose vertices were displaced by > 0.5 mm
The sculpt delta is stored in tailor_garments.sculpt_delta_json as a sparse Vec<(vertex_idx, delta_mm: [f32;3])>
A TailorGarmentSculpted event (new KernelEventType variant) is emitted after each committed brush stroke
Sculpt deltas are excluded from MeshComparator promotion equivalence checks (they are additive post-sim overrides, not solver outputs)
cargo test sculpt::test_push_displaces_vertices and sculpt::test_smooth_reduces_curvature both pass
scope.risk_if_missed: Post-simulation surface correction without re-running the full solver is the primary tool for hero-garment finessing in production. Without a sculpt tool, every surface imperfection requires a full parameter tweak, re-sim, and visual review cycle, multiplying iteration time by 5-10x for fine-detail work.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
